verses.latin_vulgate: In diebus illis iterum cum turba multa esset, nec haberent quod manducarent, convocatis discipulis, ait illis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις παμπολλοῦ ὄχλου ὄντος καὶ μὴ ἐχόντων τί φάγωσιν προσκαλεσάμενος ὁ ἰησοῦς τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ λέγει αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: In those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples *unto him*, and saith unto them,

verses.latin_vulgate: Misereor super turbam: quia ecce jam triduo sustinent me, nec habent quod manducent:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σπλαγχνίζομαι ἐπὶ τὸν ὄχλον ὅτι ἤδη ἡμέρας τρεῖς προσμένουσίν μοι καὶ οὐκ ἔχουσιν τί φάγωσιν
verses.king_james: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:

verses.latin_vulgate: et si dimisero eos jejunos in domum suam, deficient in via: quidam enim ex eis de longe venerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν ἀπολύσω αὐτοὺς νήστεις εἰς οἶκον αὐτῶν ἐκλυθήσονται ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ τινες γὰρ αὐτῶν μακρόθεν ἥκασιν
verses.king_james: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et responderunt ei discipuli sui: Unde illos quis poterit saturare panibus in solitudine?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπεκρίθησαν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ πόθεν τούτους δυνήσεταί τις ὧδε χορτάσαι ἄρτων ἐπ᾽ ἐρημίας
verses.king_james: And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these *men* with bread here in the wilderness?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogavit eos: Quot panes habetis? Qui dixerunt: Septem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπηρώτα αὐτούς πόσους ἔχετε ἄρτους οἱ δὲ εἶπον, ἑπτά
verses.king_james: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præcepit turbæ discumbere super terram. Et accipiens septem panes, gratias agens fregit, et dabat discipulis suis ut apponerent, et apposuerunt turbæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παρήγγειλεν τῷ ὄχλῳ ἀναπεσεῖν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς καὶ λαβὼν τοὺς ἑπτὰ ἄρτους εὐχαριστήσας ἔκλασεν καὶ ἐδίδου τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ἵνα παραθῶσιν καὶ παρέθηκαν τῷ ὄχλῳ
verses.king_james: And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before *them*; and they did set *them* before the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et habebant pisciculos paucos: et ipsos benedixit, et jussit apponi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶχον ἰχθύδια ὀλίγα καὶ εὐλογήσας εἶπεν παραθεῖναι καὶ αὐτὰ
verses.king_james: And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before *them*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et manducaverunt, et saturati sunt, et sustulerunt quod superaverat de fragmentis, septem sportas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφαγον δὲ, καὶ ἐχορτάσθησαν καὶ ἦραν περισσεύματα κλασμάτων ἑπτὰ σπυρίδας
verses.king_james: So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken *meat* that was left seven baskets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem qui manducaverunt, quasi quatuor millia: et dimisit eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δὲ οἱ φαγόντες ὡς τετρακισχίλιοι καὶ ἀπέλυσεν αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim ascendens navim cum discipulis suis, venit in partes Dalmanutha.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθὲως ἐμβὰς εἰς τὸ πλοῖον μετὰ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ ἦλθεν εἰς τὰ μέρη δαλμανουθά
verses.king_james: And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exierunt pharisæi, et cœperunt conquirere cum eo, quærentes ab illo signum de cælo, tentantes eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξῆλθον οἱ φαρισαῖοι καὶ ἤρξαντο συζητεῖν αὐτῷ ζητοῦντες παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ σημεῖον ἀπὸ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ πειράζοντες αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a sign from heaven, tempting him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingemiscens spiritu, ait: Quid generatio ista signum quærit? Amen dico vobis, si dabitur generationi isti signum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναστενάξας τῷ πνεύματι αὐτοῦ λέγει τί ἡ γενεὰ αὕτη σημεῖον ἐπιζητεῖ ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν εἰ δοθήσεται τῇ γενεᾷ ταύτῃ σημεῖον
verses.king_james: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dimittens eos, ascendit iterum navim et abiit trans fretum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀφεὶς αὐτοὺς ἐμβὰς πάλιν εἰς τὸ πλοῖον ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὸ πέραν
verses.king_james: And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et obliti sunt panes sumere: et nisi unum panem non habebant secum in navi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπελάθοντο λαβεῖν ἄρτους καὶ εἰ μὴ ἕνα ἄρτον οὐκ εἶχον μεθ᾽ ἑαυτῶν ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ
verses.king_james: Now *the disciples* had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præcipiebat eis, dicens: Videte, et cavete a fermento pharisæorum, et fermento Herodis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διεστέλλετο αὐτοῖς λέγων, ὁρᾶτε βλέπετε ἀπὸ τῆς ζύμης τῶν φαρισαίων καὶ τῆς ζύμης ἡρῴδου
verses.king_james: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and *of* the leaven of Herod.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cogitabant ad alterutrum, dicentes: quia panes non habemus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διελογίζοντο πρὸς ἀλλήλους λέγοντες, ὅτι ἄρτους οὐκ ἔχομεν
verses.king_james: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, *It is* because we have no bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo cognito, ait illis Jesus: Quid cogitatis, quia panes non habetis? nondum cognoscetis nec intelligitis? adhuc cæcatum habetis cor vestrum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γνοὺς ὁ ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτοῖς τί διαλογίζεσθε ὅτι ἄρτους οὐκ ἔχετε οὔπω νοεῖτε οὐδὲ συνίετε ἔτι πεπωρωμένην ἔχετε τὴν καρδίαν ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And when Jesus knew *it*, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?

verses.latin_vulgate: oculos habentes non videtis? et aures habentes non auditis? nec recordamini,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀφθαλμοὺς ἔχοντες οὐ βλέπετε καὶ ὦτα ἔχοντες οὐκ ἀκούετε καὶ οὐ μνημονεύετε
verses.king_james: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?

verses.latin_vulgate: quando quinque panes fregi in quinque millia: quot cophinos fragmentorum plenos sustulistis? Dicunt ei: Duodecim.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε τοὺς πέντε ἄρτους ἔκλασα εἰς τοὺς πεντακισχιλίους πόσους κοφίνους πλήρεις κλασμάτων ἤρατε λέγουσιν αὐτῷ δώδεκα
verses.king_james: When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quando et septem panes in quatuor millia: quot sportas fragmentorum tulistis? Et dicunt ei: Septem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ τοὺς ἑπτὰ εἰς τοὺς τετρακισχιλίους πόσων σπυρίδων πληρώματα κλασμάτων ἤρατε οἱ δὲ εἶπον, ἑπτά
verses.king_james: And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicebat eis: Quomodo nondum intelligitis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς πῶς οὐ συνίετε
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et veniunt Bethsaidam, et adducunt ei cæcum, et rogabant eum ut illum tangeret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔρχεταί εἰς βηθσαϊδάν καὶ φέρουσιν αὐτῷ τυφλὸν καὶ παρακαλοῦσιν αὐτὸν ἵνα αὐτοῦ ἅψηται
verses.king_james: And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et apprehensa manu cæci, eduxit eum extra vicum: et exspuens in oculos ejus impositis manibus suis, interrogavit eum si quid videret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπιλαβόμενος τῆς χειρὸς τοῦ τυφλοῦ ἐξήγαγεν αὐτὸν ἔξω τῆς κώμης καὶ πτύσας εἰς τὰ ὄμματα αὐτοῦ ἐπιθεὶς τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῷ ἐπηρώτα αὐτόν εἴ τι βλέπει
verses.king_james: And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et aspiciens, ait: Video homines velut arbores ambulantes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναβλέψας ἔλεγεν βλέπω τοὺς ἀνθρώπους ὅτι ὡς δένδρα ὁρῶ περιπατοῦντας
verses.king_james: And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deinde iterum imposuit manus super oculos ejus: et cœpit videre: et restitutus est ita ut clare videret omnia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶτα πάλιν ἐπέθηκεν τὰς χεῖρας ἐπὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐποίησεν αὐτὸν αναβλέψαι καὶ ἀποκατεστάθη καὶ ἐνέβλεψεν τηλαυγῶς ἅπαντας
verses.king_james: After that he put *his* hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw every man clearly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et misit illum in domum suam, dicens: Vade in domum tuam: et si in vicum introieris, nemini dixeris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπέστειλεν αὐτὸν εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ λέγων, μηδὲ εἰς τὴν κώμην εἰσέλθῃς μηδὲ εἴπης τινὶ ἐν τῇ κώμῃ
verses.king_james: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell *it* to any in the town.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egressus est Jesus, et discipuli ejus in castella Cæsareæ Philippi: et in via interrogabat discipulos suos, dicens eis: Quem me dicunt esse homines?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ εἰς τὰς κώμας καισαρείας τῆς φιλίππου καὶ ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ ἐπηρώτα τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ λέγων αὐτοῖς, τίνα με λέγουσιν οἱ ἄνθρωποι εἶναι
verses.king_james: And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Caesarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui responderunt illi, dicentes: Joannem Baptistam, alii Eliam, alii vero quasi unum de prophetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἀπεκρίθησαν, ἰωάννην τὸν βαπτιστήν καὶ ἄλλοι ἠλίαν ἄλλοι δὲ ἕνα τῶν προφητῶν
verses.king_james: And they answered, John the Baptist: but some *say*, Elias; and others, One of the prophets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dicit illis: Vos vero quem me esse dicitis? Respondens Petrus, ait ei: Tu es Christus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς λέγει αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς δὲ τίνα με λέγετε εἶναι ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ πέτρος λέγει αὐτῷ σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et comminatus est eis, ne cui dicerent de illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπετίμησεν αὐτοῖς ἵνα μηδενὶ λέγωσιν περὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cœpit docere eos quoniam oportet Filium hominis pati multa, et reprobari a senioribus, et a summis sacerdotibus et scribis, et occidi: et post tres dies resurgere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤρξατο διδάσκειν αὐτοὺς ὅτι δεῖ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου πολλὰ παθεῖν καὶ ἀποδοκιμασθῆναι ἀπὸ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων καὶ ἀρχιερέων καὶ γραμματέων καὶ ἀποκτανθῆναι καὶ μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας ἀναστῆναι
verses.king_james: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and *of* the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et palam verbum loquebatur. Et apprehendens eum Petrus, cœpit increpare eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παῤῥησίᾳ τὸν λόγον ἐλάλει καὶ προσλαβόμενος αὐτὸν ὁ πέτρος ἤρξατο ἐπιτιμᾶν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui conversus, et videns discipulos suos, comminatus est Petro, dicens: Vade retro me Satana, quoniam non sapis quæ Dei sunt, sed quæ sunt hominum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐπιστραφεὶς καὶ ἰδὼν τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ ἐπετίμησεν τῷ πέτρῳ λέγων, ὕπαγε ὀπίσω μου σατανᾶ ὅτι οὐ φρονεῖς τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ ἀλλὰ τὰ τῶν ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et convocata turba cum discipulis suis, dixit eis: Si quis vult me sequi, deneget semetipsum: et tollat crucem suam, et sequatur me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος τὸν ὄχλον σὺν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὅστις θέλει ὀπίσω μου ἐλθεῖν, ἀπαρνησάσθω ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀράτω τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκολουθείτω μοι
verses.king_james: And when he had called the people *unto him* with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim voluerit animam suam salvam facere, perdet eam: qui autem perdiderit animam suam propter me, et Evangelium, salvam faciet eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς γὰρ ἂν θέλῃ τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ σῶσαι ἀπολέσει αὐτήν ὃς δ᾽ ἂν ἀπολέσῃ τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ καὶ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου οὐτὸς σώσει αὐτήν
verses.king_james: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel's, the same shall save it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid enim proderit homini, si lucretur mundum totum et detrimentum animæ suæ faciat?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί γὰρ ὠφελήσει ἄνθρωπον ἐὰν κερδήσῃ τὸν κόσμον ὅλον καὶ ζημιωθῃ τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?

verses.latin_vulgate: Aut quid dabit homo commutationis pro anima sua?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ τί δώσει ἄνθρωπος ἀντάλλαγμα τῆς ψυχῆς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim me confusus fuerit, et verba mea in generatione ista adultera et peccatrice, et Filius hominis confundetur eum, cum venerit in gloria Patris sui cum angelis sanctis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς γὰρ ἂν ἐπαισχυνθῇ με καὶ τοὺς ἐμοὺς λόγους ἐν τῇ γενεᾷ ταύτῃ τῇ μοιχαλίδι καὶ ἁμαρτωλῷ καὶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐπαισχυνθήσεται αὐτὸν ὅταν ἔλθῃ ἐν τῇ δόξῃ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ μετὰ τῶν ἀγγέλων τῶν ἁγίων
verses.king_james: Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
